At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[ja:裏町古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11626765]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uramachi Kofun | location = {{ill|Sendai|WD=Q46747}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.222277777778|N|140.86130555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (non-existent) | built = 5th century | area = Total length: approximately 40m (estimated) | Interred_individual = Unknown | notes = None | burial goods = Bronze mirror, knife, iron arrowheads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | image = [[File:Uramachi Kofun Seidoukyo.jpg|250px]]
Bronze mirror excavated from the site | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=13|lat_sec=20.2|lon_deg=140|lon_min=51|lon_sec=40.7|map_dot_label=Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Miyagi Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|38|13|20.2|N|140|51|40.7|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Uramachi Kofun''' (裏町古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. Constructed during the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (5th century), the mound was approximately 40 meters in length, surrounded by [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and cylindrical haniwa. burial goods such as a bronze mirror, knife, and iron arrowheads were excavated from a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The site was investigated in 1972 and 1973 before being destroyed in 1973. == Overview == Uramachi Kofun was located at the base of the {{ill|Mikamine Hills|ja|三神峯丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikamine Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, facing the southern part of the Sendai Plain. At the time of excavation, the site was within Tomizawa, Uramachi, Sendai City. However, after changes in the administrative district, it is now part of Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku. The area was once dotted with small to medium-sized kofun, including Uramachi Kofun. The mound faced westward, with a keyhole type and a single row of fukiishi and possibly three rows of haniwa around the posterior mound and one row on the anterior portion. A shallow surrounding ditch, irregular in depth, was also present. A vertical pit was dug in the center of the posterior mound to house a burial chamber made of stones, which likely contained a wooden coffin. Although looted in antiquity, small finds included a bronze nyūmonkyō mirror, a knife, an iron arrowhead, and fragments of Sue pottery and Haji pottery. The kofun is dated to the mid-5th century.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.30; Fujisawa Atsushi, "Uramachi Kofun", p.252. == location == Uramachi Kofun was situated on the western edge of the Sendai Plain between the [[:en:Natori River|Natori River]] and the {{ill|Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|ja|広瀬川 (宮城県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|1=Hirose River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485581}}, closer to the Natori River. Nearby, kofun of similar map_size include {{ill|Hitotsuzuka Kofun|ja|一塚古墳|1=Hitotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuzuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|二塚古墳 (仙台市)|1=Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|lt=Futatsuzuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}, as well as the larger {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|兜塚古墳 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}. The region's largest kofun are the [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijinyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]], located across the Natori and Hirose Rivers, respectively. Uramachi Kofun was built on the southern edge of the Mikamine Hills, overlooking the lowlands. Viewed from below, the keyhole type would have appeared against the backdrop of the hills.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.17. Many similar Kofuns in the area were destroyed before receiving names or documentation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.6. == Excavation and Current Status == In 1949, the archaeologist Itō Nobuo observed the site, describing it as an overgrown mound surrounded by a cedar grove. At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

Viewed from below, the keyhole type would have appeared against the backdrop of the hills.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.17. Many similar Kofuns in the area were destroyed before receiving names or documentation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.6. == Excavation and Current Status == In 1949, the archaeologist Itō Nobuo observed the site, describing it as an overgrown mound surrounded by a cedar grove. At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}}

[[ja:裏町古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11626765]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uramachi Kofun | location = {{ill|Sendai|WD=Q46747}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.222277777778|N|140.86130555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (non-existent) | built = 5th century | area = Total length: approximately 40m (estimated) | Interred_individual = Unknown | notes = None | burial goods = Bronze mirror, knife, iron arrowheads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | image = [[File:Uramachi Kofun Seidoukyo.jpg|250px]]
Bronze mirror excavated from the site | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=13|lat_sec=20.2|lon_deg=140|lon_min=51|lon_sec=40.7|map_dot_label=Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Miyagi Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|38|13|20.2|N|140|51|40.7|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Uramachi Kofun''' (裏町古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. Constructed during the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (5th century), the mound was approximately 40 meters in length, surrounded by [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and cylindrical haniwa. burial goods such as a bronze mirror, knife, and iron arrowheads were excavated from a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The site was investigated in 1972 and 1973 before being destroyed in 1973. == Overview == Uramachi Kofun was located at the base of the {{ill|Mikamine Hills|ja|三神峯丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikamine Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, facing the southern part of the Sendai Plain. At the time of excavation, the site was within Tomizawa, Uramachi, Sendai City. However, after changes in the administrative district, it is now part of Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku. The area was once dotted with small to medium-sized kofun, including Uramachi Kofun. The mound faced westward, with a keyhole type and a single row of fukiishi and possibly three rows of haniwa around the posterior mound and one row on the anterior portion. A shallow surrounding ditch, irregular in depth, was also present. A vertical pit was dug in the center of the posterior mound to house a burial chamber made of stones, which likely contained a wooden coffin. Although looted in antiquity, small finds included a bronze nyūmonkyō mirror, a knife, an iron arrowhead, and fragments of Sue pottery and Haji pottery. The kofun is dated to the mid-5th century.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.30; Fujisawa Atsushi, "Uramachi Kofun", p.252. == location == Uramachi Kofun was situated on the western edge of the Sendai Plain between the [[:en:Natori River|Natori River]] and the {{ill|Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|ja|広瀬川 (宮城県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|1=Hirose River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485581}}, closer to the Natori River. Nearby, kofun of similar map_size include {{ill|Hitotsuzuka Kofun|ja|一塚古墳|1=Hitotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuzuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|二塚古墳 (仙台市)|1=Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|lt=Futatsuzuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}, as well as the larger {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|兜塚古墳 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}. The region's largest kofun are the [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijinyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]], located across the Natori and Hirose Rivers, respectively. Uramachi Kofun was built on the southern edge of the Mikamine Hills, overlooking the lowlands. Viewed from below, the keyhole type would have appeared against the backdrop of the hills.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.17. Many similar Kofuns in the area were destroyed before receiving names or documentation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.6. == Excavation and Current Status == In 1949, the archaeologist Itō Nobuo observed the site, describing it as an overgrown mound surrounded by a cedar grove. At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}}

The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}}

revision.text:
[[ja:裏町古墳]] [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11626765]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uramachi Kofun | location = {{ill|Sendai|WD=Q46747}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.222277777778|N|140.86130555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (non-existent) | built = 5th century | area = Total length: approximately 40m (estimated) | Interred_individual = Unknown | notes = None | burial goods = Bronze mirror, knife, iron arrowheads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | image = [[File:Uramachi Kofun Seidoukyo.jpg|250px]]
Bronze mirror excavated from the site | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=13|lat_sec=20.2|lon_deg=140|lon_min=51|lon_sec=40.7|map_dot_label=Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Miyagi Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|38|13|20.2|N|140|51|40.7|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Uramachi Kofun''' (裏町古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. Constructed during the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (5th century), the mound was approximately 40 meters in length, surrounded by [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and cylindrical haniwa. burial goods such as a bronze mirror, knife, and iron arrowheads were excavated from a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The site was investigated in 1972 and 1973 before being destroyed in 1973. == Overview == Uramachi Kofun was located at the base of the {{ill|Mikamine Hills|ja|三神峯丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikamine Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, facing the southern part of the Sendai Plain. At the time of excavation, the site was within Tomizawa, Uramachi, Sendai City. However, after changes in the administrative district, it is now part of Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku. The area was once dotted with small to medium-sized kofun, including Uramachi Kofun. The mound faced westward, with a keyhole type and a single row of fukiishi and possibly three rows of haniwa around the posterior mound and one row on the anterior portion. A shallow surrounding ditch, irregular in depth, was also present. A vertical pit was dug in the center of the posterior mound to house a burial chamber made of stones, which likely contained a wooden coffin. Although looted in antiquity, small finds included a bronze nyūmonkyō mirror, a knife, an iron arrowhead, and fragments of Sue pottery and Haji pottery. The kofun is dated to the mid-5th century.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.30; Fujisawa Atsushi, "Uramachi Kofun", p.252. == location == Uramachi Kofun was situated on the western edge of the Sendai Plain between the [[:en:Natori River|Natori River]] and the {{ill|Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|ja|広瀬川 (宮城県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|1=Hirose River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485581}}, closer to the Natori River. Nearby, kofun of similar map_size include {{ill|Hitotsuzuka Kofun|ja|一塚古墳|1=Hitotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuzuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|二塚古墳 (仙台市)|1=Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|lt=Futatsuzuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}, as well as the larger {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|兜塚古墳 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}. The region's largest kofun are the [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijinyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]], located across the Natori and Hirose Rivers, respectively. Uramachi Kofun was built on the southern edge of the Mikamine Hills, overlooking the lowlands. Viewed from below, the keyhole type would have appeared against the backdrop of the hills.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.17. Many similar Kofuns in the area were destroyed before receiving names or documentation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.6. == Excavation and Current Status == In 1949, the archaeologist Itō Nobuo observed the site, describing it as an overgrown mound surrounded by a cedar grove. At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:裏町古墳]] [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11626765]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uramachi Kofun | location = {{ill|Sendai|WD=Q46747}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.222277777778|N|140.86130555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (non-existent) | built = 5th century | area = Total length: approximately 40m (estimated) | Interred_individual = Unknown | notes = None | burial goods = Bronze mirror, knife, iron arrowheads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | image = [[File:Uramachi Kofun Seidoukyo.jpg|250px]]
Bronze mirror excavated from the site | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=13|lat_sec=20.2|lon_deg=140|lon_min=51|lon_sec=40.7|map_dot_label=Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Miyagi Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|38|13|20.2|N|140|51|40.7|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Uramachi Kofun''' (裏町古墳) was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. Constructed during the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (5th century), the mound was approximately 40 meters in length, surrounded by [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and cylindrical haniwa. burial goods such as a bronze mirror, knife, and iron arrowheads were excavated from a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The site was investigated in 1972 and 1973 before being destroyed in 1973. == Overview == Uramachi Kofun was located at the base of the {{ill|Mikamine Hills|ja|三神峯丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikamine Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, facing the southern part of the Sendai Plain. At the time of excavation, the site was within Tomizawa, Uramachi, Sendai City. However, after changes in the administrative district, it is now part of Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku. The area was once dotted with small to medium-sized kofun, including Uramachi Kofun. The mound faced westward, with a keyhole type and a single row of fukiishi and possibly three rows of haniwa around the posterior mound and one row on the anterior portion. A shallow surrounding ditch, irregular in depth, was also present. A vertical pit was dug in the center of the posterior mound to house a burial chamber made of stones, which likely contained a wooden coffin. Although looted in antiquity, small finds included a bronze nyūmonkyō mirror, a knife, an iron arrowhead, and fragments of Sue pottery and Haji pottery. The kofun is dated to the mid-5th century.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.30; Fujisawa Atsushi, "Uramachi Kofun", p.252. == location == Uramachi Kofun was situated on the western edge of the Sendai Plain between the [[:en:Natori River|Natori River]] and the {{ill|Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|ja|広瀬川 (宮城県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|1=Hirose River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485581}}, closer to the Natori River. Nearby, kofun of similar map_size include {{ill|Hitotsuzuka Kofun|ja|一塚古墳|1=Hitotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuzuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|二塚古墳 (仙台市)|1=Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|lt=Futatsuzuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}, as well as the larger {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|兜塚古墳 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}. The region's largest kofun are the [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijinyama Kofun]] and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]], located across the Natori and Hirose Rivers, respectively. Uramachi Kofun was built on the southern edge of the Mikamine Hills, overlooking the lowlands. Viewed from below, the keyhole type would have appeared against the backdrop of the hills.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.17. Many similar Kofuns in the area were destroyed before receiving names or documentation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.6. == Excavation and Current Status == In 1949, the archaeologist Itō Nobuo observed the site, describing it as an overgrown mound surrounded by a cedar grove. At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[ja:裏町古墳]] [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11626765]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uramachi Kofun | location = {{ill|Sendai|WD=Q46747}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.222277777778|N|140.86130555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (non-existent) | built = 5th century | area = Total length: approximately 40m (estimated) | Interred_individual = Unknown | notes = None | burial goods = Bronze mirror, knife, iron arrowheads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | image = [[File:Uramachi Kofun Seidoukyo.jpg|250px]]
Bronze mirror excavated from the site | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=13|lat_sec=20.2|lon_deg=140|lon_min=51|lon_sec=40.7|map_dot_label=Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Miyagi Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|38|13|20.2|N|140|51|40.7|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Uramachi Kofun''' (裏町古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. Constructed during the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (5th century), the mound was approximately 40 meters in length, surrounded by {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} and cylindrical haniwa. burial goods such as a bronze mirror, knife, and iron arrowheads were excavated from a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The site was investigated in 1972 and 1973 before being destroyed in 1973. == Overview == Uramachi Kofun was located at the base of the {{ill|Mikamine Hills|ja|三神峯丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikamine Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, facing the southern part of the Sendai Plain. At the time of excavation, the site was within Tomizawa, Uramachi, Sendai City. However, after changes in the administrative district, it is now part of Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku. The area was once dotted with small to medium-sized kofun, including Uramachi Kofun. The mound faced westward, with a keyhole type and a single row of fukiishi and possibly three rows of haniwa around the posterior mound and one row on the anterior portion. A shallow surrounding ditch, irregular in depth, was also present. A vertical pit was dug in the center of the posterior mound to house a burial chamber made of stones, which likely contained a wooden coffin. Although looted in antiquity, small finds included a bronze nyūmonkyō mirror, a knife, an iron arrowhead, and fragments of Sue pottery and Haji pottery. The kofun is dated to the mid-5th century.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.30; Fujisawa Atsushi, "Uramachi Kofun", p.252. == location == Uramachi Kofun was situated on the western edge of the Sendai Plain between the {{ill|Natori River|en|Natori River|WD=Q11414763|lt=Natori River}} and the {{ill|Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|ja|広瀬川 (宮城県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|1=Hirose River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485581}}, closer to the Natori River. Nearby, kofun of similar map_size include {{ill|Hitotsuzuka Kofun|ja|一塚古墳|1=Hitotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuzuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|二塚古墳 (仙台市)|1=Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|lt=Futatsuzuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}, as well as the larger {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|兜塚古墳 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}. The region's largest kofun are the {{ill|Raijinyama Kofun|en|Raijinyama Kofun|WD=Q11660187|lt=Raijinyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|en|Tōmizuka Kofun|WD=Q11642380|lt=Tōmizuka Kofun}}, located across the Natori and Hirose Rivers, respectively. Uramachi Kofun was built on the southern edge of the Mikamine Hills, overlooking the lowlands. Viewed from below, the keyhole type would have appeared against the backdrop of the hills.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.17. Many similar Kofuns in the area were destroyed before receiving names or documentation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.6. == Excavation and Current Status == In 1949, the archaeologist Itō Nobuo observed the site, describing it as an overgrown mound surrounded by a cedar grove. At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Bronze mirror excavated from the site | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=13|lat_sec=20.2|lon_deg=140|lon_min=51|lon_sec=40.7|map_dot_label=Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Miyagi Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|38|13|20.2|N|140|51|40.7|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Uramachi Kofun''' (裏町古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uramachi Kofun | location = {{ill|Sendai|WD=Q46747}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.222277777778|N|140.86130555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (non-existent) | built = 5th century | area = Total length: approximately 40m (estimated) | Interred_individual = Unknown | notes = None | burial goods = Bronze mirror, knife, iron arrowheads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | image = [[File:Uramachi Kofun Seidoukyo.jpg|250px]]
Bronze mirror excavated from the site | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=13|lat_sec=20.2|lon_deg=140|lon_min=51|lon_sec=40.7|map_dot_label=Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Miyagi Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|38|13|20.2|N|140|51|40.7|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Uramachi Kofun''' (裏町古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. Constructed during the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (5th century), the mound was approximately 40 meters in length, surrounded by {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} and cylindrical haniwa. burial goods such as a bronze mirror, knife, and iron arrowheads were excavated from a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The site was investigated in 1972 and 1973 before being destroyed in 1973. == Overview == Uramachi Kofun was located at the base of the {{ill|Mikamine Hills|ja|三神峯丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikamine Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, facing the southern part of the Sendai Plain. At the time of excavation, the site was within Tomizawa, Uramachi, Sendai City. However, after changes in the administrative district, it is now part of Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku. The area was once dotted with small to medium-sized kofun, including Uramachi Kofun. The mound faced westward, with a keyhole type and a single row of fukiishi and possibly three rows of haniwa around the posterior mound and one row on the anterior portion. A shallow surrounding ditch, irregular in depth, was also present. A vertical pit was dug in the center of the posterior mound to house a burial chamber made of stones, which likely contained a wooden coffin. Although looted in antiquity, small finds included a bronze nyūmonkyō mirror, a knife, an iron arrowhead, and fragments of Sue pottery and Haji pottery. The kofun is dated to the mid-5th century.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.30; Fujisawa Atsushi, "Uramachi Kofun", p.252. == location == Uramachi Kofun was situated on the western edge of the Sendai Plain between the {{ill|Natori River|en|Natori River|WD=Q11414763|lt=Natori River}} and the {{ill|Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|ja|広瀬川 (宮城県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|1=Hirose River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485581}}, closer to the Natori River. Nearby, kofun of similar map_size include {{ill|Hitotsuzuka Kofun|ja|一塚古墳|1=Hitotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuzuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|二塚古墳 (仙台市)|1=Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|lt=Futatsuzuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}, as well as the larger {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|兜塚古墳 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}. The region's largest kofun are the {{ill|Raijinyama Kofun|en|Raijinyama Kofun|WD=Q11660187|lt=Raijinyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|en|Tōmizuka Kofun|WD=Q11642380|lt=Tōmizuka Kofun}}, located across the Natori and Hirose Rivers, respectively. Uramachi Kofun was built on the southern edge of the Mikamine Hills, overlooking the lowlands. Viewed from below, the keyhole type would have appeared against the backdrop of the hills.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.17. Many similar Kofuns in the area were destroyed before receiving names or documentation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.6. == Excavation and Current Status == In 1949, the archaeologist Itō Nobuo observed the site, describing it as an overgrown mound surrounded by a cedar grove. At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

The kofun is dated to the mid-5th century.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.30; Fujisawa Atsushi, "Uramachi Kofun", p.252. == location == Uramachi Kofun was situated on the western edge of the Sendai Plain between the {{ill|Natori River|en|Natori River|WD=Q11414763|lt=Natori River|1=Natori River}} and the {{ill|Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|ja|広瀬川 (宮城県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|1=Hirose River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485581}}, closer to the Natori River. Nearby, kofun of similar map_size include {{ill|Hitotsuzuka Kofun|ja|一塚古墳|1=Hitotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuzuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|二塚古墳 (仙台市)|1=Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|lt=Futatsuzuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}, as well as the larger {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|兜塚古墳 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}. The region's largest kofun are the {{ill|Raijinyama Kofun|en|Raijinyama Kofun|WD=Q11660187|lt=Raijinyama Kofun|1=Raijinyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|en|Tōmizuka Kofun|WD=Q11642380|lt=Tōmizuka Kofun|1=Tōmizuka Kofun}}, located across the Natori and Hirose Rivers, respectively. Uramachi Kofun was built on the southern edge of the Mikamine Hills, overlooking the lowlands. Viewed from below, the keyhole type would have appeared against the backdrop of the hills.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.17. Many similar Kofuns in the area were destroyed before receiving names or documentation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.6. == Excavation and Current Status == In 1949, the archaeologist Itō Nobuo observed the site, describing it as an overgrown mound surrounded by a cedar grove. At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[ja:裏町古墳]] [[ja:裏町古墳]] [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uramachi Kofun | location = {{ill|Sendai|WD=Q46747|1=Sendai}} | coordinates = {{Coord|38.222277777778|N|140.86130555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (non-existent) | built = 5th century | area = Total length: approximately 40m (estimated) | Interred_individual = Unknown | notes = None | burial goods = Bronze mirror, knife, iron arrowheads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | image = [[File:Uramachi Kofun Seidoukyo.jpg|250px]]
Bronze mirror excavated from the site | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=13|lat_sec=20.2|lon_deg=140|lon_min=51|lon_sec=40.7|map_dot_label=Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Miyagi Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|38|13|20.2|N|140|51|40.7|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Uramachi Kofun''' (裏町古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. Constructed during the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (5th century), the mound was approximately 40 meters in length, surrounded by {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and cylindrical haniwa. burial goods such as a bronze mirror, knife, and iron arrowheads were excavated from a {{ill|pit-style burial chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. The site was investigated in 1972 and 1973 before being destroyed in 1973. == Overview == Uramachi Kofun was located at the base of the {{ill|Mikamine Hills|ja|三神峯丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikamine Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, facing the southern part of the Sendai Plain. At the time of excavation, the site was within Tomizawa, Uramachi, Sendai City. However, after changes in the administrative district, it is now part of Nishitaga 1-chome, Taihaku-ku. The area was once dotted with small to medium-sized kofun, including Uramachi Kofun. The mound faced westward, with a keyhole type and a single row of fukiishi and possibly three rows of haniwa around the posterior mound and one row on the anterior portion. A shallow surrounding ditch, irregular in depth, was also present. A vertical pit was dug in the center of the posterior mound to house a burial chamber made of stones, which likely contained a wooden coffin. Although looted in antiquity, small finds included a bronze nyūmonkyō mirror, a knife, an iron arrowhead, and fragments of Sue pottery and Haji pottery. The kofun is dated to the mid-5th century.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.30; Fujisawa Atsushi, "Uramachi Kofun", p.252. == location == Uramachi Kofun was situated on the western edge of the Sendai Plain between the {{ill|Natori River|en|Natori River|WD=Q11414763|lt=Natori River|1=Natori River}} and the {{ill|Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|ja|広瀬川 (宮城県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|1=Hirose River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485581}}, closer to the Natori River. Nearby, kofun of similar map_size include {{ill|Hitotsuzuka Kofun|ja|一塚古墳|1=Hitotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuzuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitotsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|二塚古墳 (仙台市)|1=Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|lt=Futatsuzuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsuzuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}, as well as the larger {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|ja|兜塚古墳 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Sendai)|qq=}}. The region's largest kofun are the {{ill|Raijinyama Kofun|en|Raijinyama Kofun|WD=Q11660187|lt=Raijinyama Kofun|1=Raijinyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|en|Tōmizuka Kofun|WD=Q11642380|lt=Tōmizuka Kofun|1=Tōmizuka Kofun}}, located across the Natori and Hirose Rivers, respectively. Uramachi Kofun was built on the southern edge of the Mikamine Hills, overlooking the lowlands. Viewed from below, the keyhole type would have appeared against the backdrop of the hills.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.17. Many similar Kofuns in the area were destroyed before receiving names or documentation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', p.6. == Excavation and Current Status == In 1949, the archaeologist Itō Nobuo observed the site, describing it as an overgrown mound surrounded by a cedar grove. At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

At that time, Itō believed it to be a circular mound. By 1972, the area had been transtypeed into residential lots, and the grove had been cleared. A cultural property survey by the Sendai City Board of Education revealed the keyhole type. The mound was partially destroyed during construction and was entirely removed in 1973 following excavation.'Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report', pp.1, 7-10. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sendai City Board of Education|title=Sendai City Tomizawa Uramachi Kofun Excavation Report (Cultural Property Investigation Report No. 7)|publisher=Sendai City|year=1974}} * {{Cite book|author=Itō Nobuo|title=Ancient Sites in Sendai|publisher=Sendai City History Compilation Committee|year=1950}} * {{Cite book|author=Fujisawa Atsushi|title=The Bronze Mirror from Uramachi Kofun|publisher=Sendai City Board of Education|journal=Annual Report|volume=9|year=1988}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000021.html Uramachi Kofun - Sendai City Cultural Property] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|裏町古墳|version=104230622|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:裏町古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11626765}} [[Category:History of Taihaku Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyagi prefecture]] [[Category:Taihaku-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
